votes.reason: The Python code accurately translates the Coq logic, including the prime checking, string splitting, filtering, and joining operations.
The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification, including the prime checking logic, word splitting, joining, and preconditions.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification, including the prime checking logic, string splitting, filtering by prime length, and joining. The precondition and specification functions also match exactly.

The Python code correctly implements the same logic as the Coq code. The has_divisor_from function iteratively checks divisors from d down to 2, matching the Coq recursive structure. is_prime_bool handles 0,1 as non-prime and checks for divisors. split_words correctly splits on spaces, join_words joins with spaces, and words_in_sentence_impl filters words with prime lengths. The precondition and spec match the Coq definitions. All concrete inputs will produce the same results.

The Python code faithfully implements the same logic as the Coq code. The has_divisor_from function iteratively checks divisors from d down to 2, matching the Coq recursive structure. is_prime_bool handles 0 and 1 as non-prime and checks for divisors from n-1 down to 2. split_words splits on spaces identically (the Coq version reverses accumulated characters, but Python appends directly which achieves the same result). join_words concatenates words with spaces between them. The precondition checks length bounds and character validity (letters or space) identically. The spec checks output equality with the implementation result.

The Python code correctly implements the same logic as the Coq code. The has_divisor_from function iteratively checks divisors from d down to 2, matching the Coq recursive structure. is_prime_bool handles 0 and 1 as non-prime and checks for divisors from n-1 down to 2. split_words splits on spaces identically (the Coq version uses rev on accumulated characters, but Python appends directly which produces the same result). join_words concatenates words with spaces between them. The precondition and spec check the same constraints and equality. All concrete inputs will produce the same results.

Python mirrors Coq: same prime check via descending divisors, same split on spaces ignoring empties, same join with single spaces, and same filtering by prime word lengths. Precondition checks and spec equality also match for ASCII letters/spaces.
Prime test, word splitting/joining, filtering by prime lengths, and pre/spec checks are implemented identically; outputs match on all concrete inputs.
Python mirrors Coq: same prime test, same split on spaces ignoring multiples, same join with single spaces, and the pre/spec checks match on allowed ASCII inputs.
